Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

I can change this once the tests have passed.

Patch Set 1:

> Trevis Autotest retesting enforced review-dne-part-6 session on
 > el8.8-x86_64/ldiskfs servers and clients for build 96604 patch 1.
 > Requested by HUANG XING because of SeemsNoCrash.

This was LU-16982:

 [16987.849078] Lustre: server umount lustre-MDT0003 complete
 [16988.798971] ------------[ cut here ]------------
 [16988.800061] kernel BUG at fs/jbd2/transaction.c:378!

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

In the long run, we may group these reparation functions into a struct, and call different functions in dryrun mode which should only print messages about the error to fix.

Patch Set 1:

> In the long run, we may group these reparation functions into a
 > struct, and call different functions in dryrun mode which should
 > only print messages about the error to fix.

yes, one of the user complaints about LFSCK is that it doesn't print very useful information about what errors it finds and what it has fixed.   Using "printk=+lfsck" is _close_ to useful, but in some cases is much too verbose.  It would be better to have "lctl lfsck_start" run debug_daemon in the background and log LFSCK messages into a separate log file.

parents.subject: LU-17000 lnet: fix use-after-free in lnet_startup_lndnet

commit.subject: LU-17010 lfsck: don't create trans in dryrun mode
commit.message:
LU-17010 lfsck: don't create trans in dryrun mode

In LFSCK, the LFSCK transaction should not be created in
dryrun mode, which is related to the following patch,

Fixes: 0c1ae1cb9c19 ("LU-13124 scrub: check for multiple linked file")
Change-Id: Id543bc3c0e300c1cc14b670d724ebcacac3bf71b
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51849
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
